---
pagetitle: "Hello World - Generality Labs"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: custom
    theme: none
    include-in-header: ../../../_includes/head.html
    include-before-body:
      - ../../../_includes/post-before.html
      - _header.html
    include-after-body: ../../../_includes/post-after.html
    link-external-newwindow: true
    embed-resources: false
---

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
gl = import("/assets/gl-components/gl.js")
mc = import("/assets/gl-components/model-colors.js")
```

Hello, world. This post exists to prove the shared scaffolding works end to end: the nav, typography, and footer come from the site-wide includes, and the figure below is rendered client-side by an OJS cell calling the house component library.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
letters = {
  const counts = {};
  for (const ch of "helloworld") counts[ch] = (counts[ch] ?? 0) + 1;
  return Object.entries(counts).map(([label, value]) => ({ label, value, group: "letter" }));
}
gl.glBars({Plot}, letters, {
  xLabel: "occurrences",
  caption: "Letter frequency in “hello world”.",
  width: 688,
})
```

Everything a real post needs works from here: markdown prose, `inline code`, [links](https://generality.org), and OJS cells reading data contracts from a local `data/` directory.
